scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all chord tones (C, E, G) plus safe extensions (D, A). The most foolproof scale for C major improvisation with zero avoid notes.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Target the root (C) and major 3rd (E) for resolution. The A (13th) adds brightness. Perfect for simple, memorable melodies.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The go-to scale for C major in rock, country, blues, and pop. Guaranteed consonance. Ideal for beginners and memorable hooks.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The fundamental scale of C major. No sharps or flats - the natural scale. Contains all chord tones and available extensions for complete melodic freedom.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work over I major. The F (11th) can sound suspended - resolve to E or G. Emphasize chord tones on downbeats.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Complete freedom over C major. The foundation of Western music. Works in classical, pop, jazz, folk, and all styles.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Raises F to F# (#11) creating dreamy, ethereal major sound. The characteristic Lydian sound - bright, floating, modern.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the #11 (F#) for signature Lydian color. Creates uplifting, optimistic quality. Perfect for sustained I major chords.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for Cmaj7#11 or modern I major sounds. Essential for film scores, jazz, fusion, and progressive rock. Creates magical, floating quality.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Lowers B to Bb (b7) creating dominant 7th character. Perfect for bluesy, rock-oriented C major. The I7 sound common in blues.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The Bb (b7) creates bluesy edge. Emphasize E and Bb for dominant character. Great for rock and blues guitar.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for C7 chords and blues/rock vamps. Essential for jam bands, folk rock, and Americana. Use when you want major with bluesy flavor.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Built on V - creates bluesy major sound
scaleCompatibility.rationale: All notes diatonic to C major. The minor pentatonic from the V creates safe blues-rock sound over I major.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Creates bluesy major without clash. The Bb adds slight tension. Classic rock guitar approach to major chords.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential rock guitar scale over C major. Safe and bluesy. Perfect for rock solos in major keys from Led Zeppelin to modern rock.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. The B is a chord tone (maj7), making C Ionian the natural choice for Cmaj7 chords.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the maj7 (B) for sophisticated color. The F (11th) can be used but resolve to E or G. All tones work beautifully.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Complete freedom over Cmaj7. The quintessential maj7 scale. Perfect for jazz, pop, R&B, and contemporary music.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The #4 (F#) avoids suspended sound of natural 11th. This is THE scale for Imaj7 in modern jazz and fusion.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 (F#) is signature Lydian sound. Creates floating, dreamy quality. Essential for Cmaj7#11 voicings.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The definitive modern Imaj7 scale. Essential for jazz, fusion, film music, and progressive styles. Creates sophisticated, ethereal maj7 color.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Omits 4th and 7th for core consonance. Simpler maj7 approach perfect for melodic clarity and pop contexts.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Very safe and melodic. Implies maj7 without stating it. Great for hooks and singable lines over Cmaj7.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for pop and R&B maj7 approaches. Creates smooth melodies without harmonic complexity. Ideal for vocal lines and memorable hooks.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: D Dorian is the natural ii mode in C major. The major 6th (B) creates brighter, jazzier minor sound essential for ii chords.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the B (13th) for Dorian character. Essential for ii-V-I progressions (Dm7-G7-Cmaj7). Smooth voice leading.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The definitive ii chord scale. Essential for jazz, pop, R&B, and neo-soul. Perfect for Dm7-G7-Cmaj7 progressions.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Natural minor with Bb instead of B. Creates traditional, somber minor sound. More melancholic than Dorian.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The Bb (b13) creates darker color. Use for traditional sad minor sound. Common in rock ballads and classical.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for darker ii chord sound. Perfect for melancholic sections. More traditional than Dorian. Common in emotional rock and classical contexts.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Safe minor pentatonic removes harmonic complexity. Universal minor sound without modal color.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Very safe and simple. Perfect for straightforward minor melodies. Great for beginners and bluesy approaches.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for simple, bluesy ii minor. Works in rock, blues, and pop. Safe choice for improvisation without modal sophistication.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. The major 6th (B) is essential for ii-V-I. The standard jazz iim7 sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The B (13th) is crucial for Dorian color. Smooth voice leading to G7. This IS the ii-V-I sound in jazz.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for Dm7-G7-Cmaj7. The definitive iim7 scale in jazz, R&B, neo-soul, and contemporary music. Non-negotiable for jazz harmony.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Thinking in C major reinforces key center. All notes work since Dm7 is diatonic to C. Simple and effective.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think C major, emphasize D as root. Maintains tonal clarity. Perfect for understanding diatonic relationships.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for key center emphasis. Great for teaching and maintaining tonal awareness. Works universally in all contexts.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Removes complexity while maintaining minor character. Safe but lacks Dorian sophistication.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Very safe but simple. Won't clash but lacks the hip Dorian sound. Good for pop and rock contexts.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for simple pop/rock approaches. Great for beginners. Use when you want uncomplicated minor sound.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural iii mode with exotic b2
scaleCompatibility.rationale: E Phrygian is the iii mode of C major. The b2 (F) creates Spanish/exotic character. Less common but effective for drama.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the b2 (F) for Phrygian color. Creates dark, mysterious quality. Perfect for tension and exotic flavor.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for exotic, Spanish, or metal-influenced iii minor. Creates drama in C major. Common in progressive rock and flamenco.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Natural 2nd (F#) is brighter than Phrygian's b2. More conventional minor sound for iii chord.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: More straightforward than Phrygian. Less exotic, more conventional. F# creates smoother melodic motion.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for traditional minor sound on iii. Less dramatic than Phrygian. Works for conventional minor passages.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: All C major notes work over Em since it's diatonic. Maintains key center. Simple and effective.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think C major, emphasize E as root. The F creates natural Phrygian flavor. Maintains tonal clarity.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for maintaining key center over iii. Simple diatonic approach. Works universally.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural iii mode with characteristic b2
scaleCompatibility.rationale: All four chord tones present. The b2 (F) creates exotic Phrygian character for iiim7 in major keys.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The b9 (F) creates characteristic tension. Use for dark, dramatic, or Spanish passages. Creates exotic color in C major.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for exotic iiim7 color. Use in progressive rock, metal, Spanish music. Less common but highly effective for drama.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Simple parent scale approach. All notes work since Em7 is diatonic to C major. Maintains key center.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think C major, emphasize E as root. The F creates Phrygian flavor naturally. Simple and effective.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for straightforward diatonic iiim7. Perfect for maintaining key awareness. Works in all styles.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: F major is the natural scale for IV in C major. Creates subdominant function essential for I-IV-V progressions.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize root (F) and major 3rd (A). The Bb (11th) can sound suspended - resolve to A or C. Classic subdominant sound.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for I-IV-V progressions. Perfect for folk, rock, country, and pop. The subdominant is fundamental to Western harmony.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The #4 (B natural) creates modern, floating IV sound. Avoids suspended sound of natural 11th. Common in jazz and film music.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 (B) creates bright, dreamy quality. Perfect for Fmaj7#11. Very common in contemporary IV chord harmony.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated, modern IV sound. Perfect for jazz, fusion, R&B, and film music. Creates floating subdominant quality.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Removes 4th and 7th for guaranteed consonance. Perfect for country, folk, and rock. Simple and singable.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Cannot clash - all notes perfectly consonant. Great for melodies and hooks. Lack of Bb avoids suspended sound.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for country, folk, and rock IV chords. Ideal for memorable melodies. Essential for singer-songwriter and accessible styles.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: C major scale over IV maintains key center. All notes work since F is IV of C. Simple diatonic thinking.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think C major, emphasize F as root. Maintains tonal clarity. All diatonic tones work perfectly.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for diatonic IV chord approach. Maintains key awareness. Works universally in all styles.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Lydian is THE choice for IVmaj7 in jazz. The #11 (B) avoids suspended sound and creates bright, modern character.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 (B) is signature sound. Creates floating, dreamy quality. This is THE definitive IVmaj7 sound in jazz and fusion.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for modern IVmaj7. Perfect for jazz, fusion, R&B, and contemporary pop. Use for Fmaj7#11. Creates sophisticated subdominant.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: F Ionian provides all chord tones. The natural 11th (Bb) can sound suspended but works in traditional contexts.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Use with care - natural 11th (Bb) can clash. Resolve Bb to A or C. Better for traditional contexts.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for traditional IVmaj7 sound. Works in folk, country, and classical. More conventional than Lydian.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: C major contains all IVmaj7 tones. Simple diatonic thinking. Maintains key center. Notice the B creates natural Lydian flavor.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think C major, emphasize F as root. The B creates natural Lydian quality. Simple and effective.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for maintaining key center over IVmaj7. Works universally. Natural Lydian quality from diatonic thinking.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural V chord mode in C major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: G Mixolydian shares all notes with C major. The b7 (F) creates dominant 7th character. Perfect for V-I resolution.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The F (b7) creates dominant pull to C. Emphasize B (3rd) and F (b7) for strong V-I. The tritone resolves outward.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for V7 chords in C major. Perfect for dominant-tonic resolution. Use for blues, rock, funk, and all dominant situations.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: G major provides pure V triad without 7th. The F# (maj7) is brighter than Mixolydian's F. More classical V-I sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: More classical V approach. The F# creates major 7th rather than dominant 7th. Perfect for traditional V-I cadences.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for classical, traditional, or pure major V sound. Perfect for hymns, classical music, and traditional harmony.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Removes complexity while maintaining dominant character. All notes safe and consonant. Perfect for country and rock.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Cannot clash - perfectly consonant. Great for simple, memorable V melodies. Perfect for country and folk.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for country, folk, and rock V chords. Ideal for simple, singable melodies. Essential for accessible styles.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: C major contains all V chord tones. G Mixolydian = C Ionian starting on G. Simple key center thinking.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think C major, emphasize G as root. The F creates natural Mixolydian/dominant character. Maintains key focus.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for diatonic V approach. Maintains key center awareness. Works universally in all contexts.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones perfectly. The b7 (F) is a chord tone. Essential for V7-I resolution in all styles.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize 3rd (B) and b7 (F) - the tritone that resolves to C. These create dominant character and pull to C major.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for all V7 situations. Perfect for ii-V-I (Dm7-G7-Cmaj7). Use in jazz, rock, blues, pop, and all dominant harmony.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: C major over G7 maintains key center. G Mixolydian and C Ionian are the same notes. Simple and effective.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think C major, emphasize G as root. The F creates dominant 7th automatically. Maintains clear tonal direction to C.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for maintaining key awareness over V7. Works universally. Great for teaching diatonic harmony.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Adds blue notes for authentic blues dominant. The b5 (Db) creates characteristic blues tension. Essential for blues and rock.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Use Db (b5) as passing tone to D. Creates authentic blues sound. The minor 3rd (Bb) adds bluesy tension.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for blues and blues-rock G7. Perfect for 12-bar blues. Use when you want gritty, authentic blues dominant.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains b9, #9, #11, b13 for maximum tension. Used in jazz for dramatic V7 resolution. Very dissonant, modern sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Maximum tension for dramatic jazz resolutions. All alterations create strong pull to C. Very advanced, bebop sound.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for modern jazz G7alt chords. Perfect for dramatic V7-I in bebop, post-bop, and fusion. Very advanced.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Relative minor - shares all notes with C major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: A minor is the vi of C major. Shares all seven notes with C major. The most common minor chord in C major progressions.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All C major notes work perfectly. This is the natural minor sound in the key. Perfect for vi-IV-I-V progressions.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for vi chords in C major. Perfect for C-G-Am-F, Am-F-C-G progressions. Works in pop, rock, folk, and all styles.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Dorian adds F# (major 6th) for brighter minor. More sophisticated than natural minor. Common in jazz on vi chords.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The F# (major 6th) creates jazzy, bright minor. More modern than Aeolian. Perfect for sophisticated vi harmony.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated, jazzy vi minor. Perfect for jazz, funk, R&B, and neo-soul. Brighter alternative to natural minor.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Removes complexity for guaranteed consonance. Perfect for memorable melodies and simple improvisation.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Cannot clash - all notes safe. Perfect for singable melodies. Great for pop and rock vi minor.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for simple, memorable vi minor melodies. Ideal for pop, rock, singer-songwriter. Essential for accessible lines.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: C major and A minor share all notes. A Aeolian = C Ionian starting on A. Maintains key center awareness.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think C major, emphasize A as root. Maintains tonal clarity. All notes work perfectly for diatonic vi minor.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for understanding relative major/minor. Works universally. Great for teaching and key center focus.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. The natural minor sound is perfect for vi minor 7. Most common vim7 choice.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work beautifully. This is the standard vim7 sound. Perfect for emotional, melancholic sections.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for vim7 in C major. Perfect for C-G-Am7-F progressions. Works in pop, rock, folk, and all styles.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The major 6th (F#) creates brighter, more sophisticated minor 7. Common in jazz and R&B for vim7.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The F# (13th) creates jazz flavor. More sophisticated than natural minor. Perfect for R&B and neo-soul vim7.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated, modern vim7. Perfect for jazz, R&B, neo-soul, and contemporary pop. Brighter than Aeolian.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Simple and effective. All C major notes work over Am7. A Aeolian = C Ionian. Maintains key center.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think C major, emphasize A as root. Maintains tonal clarity. Perfect for diatonic relationships.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for diatonic vim7 approach. Maintains key awareness. Works universally in all contexts.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: B Locrian is the vii° mode in C major. Contains all diminished chord tones. Creates leading tone function to I.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Highly unstable - resolves strongly to C. The B is leading tone to C. Use for dramatic tension before I resolution.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use sparingly for leading tone tension. Creates strong pull to I. Common in classical and jazz for dramatic resolutions.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Whole-half diminished provides tensions for diminished chords. Symmetrical construction. Advanced jazz concept.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Very dissonant and tense. Use for maximum tension before resolution. Creates symmetrical, angular melodies.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for advanced jazz vii° approaches. Very dissonant. Perfect for bebop and modern jazz. Maximum tension.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Source of vii° in minor keys
scaleCompatibility.rationale: Bdim comes from C harmonic minor. Creates classical minor key resolution. The B is raised 7th of C minor.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Classical approach to diminished. The B leads to C in minor contexts. Traditional classical resolution.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for classical or neoclassical approaches. Perfect for minor key contexts. Traditional leading tone resolution.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: B Locrian contains all four chord tones. The vii mode naturally produces m7b5. Essential for diatonic half-diminished chords.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Very unstable - creates strong pull to C. The B leads to C. Use for tension before resolution. b5 and b9 create dissonance.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for viim7b5 in major keys. Used in jazz ii-V-I (Bm7b5-E7-Am) in minor. Creates leading tone tension.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: C major contains all notes of Bm7b5. B Locrian = C Ionian starting on B. Simple diatonic thinking.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think C major, emphasize B as root. Creates natural Locrian sound. Maintains key center awareness.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for diatonic understanding of viim7b5. Maintains key connection. Works for teaching and practical application.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: The foundation of Western music. The most fundamental three-chord progression in rock, blues, and country.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Strong, resolute, optimistic. Creates clear sense of home (I), departure (IV), tension (V), and resolution.
famousSongs: La Bamba, Twist and Shout, Wild Thing, Gloria
commonProgressions.tips: The most fundamental progression. Perfect for beginners. In C, no black keys needed. Can add 7ths (C-F-G7) for more color.

commonProgressions.name: I-V-vi-IV
commonProgressions.description: The modern pop progression. Also called 'Axis of Awesome' - dominates contemporary pop music.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Emotionally versatile - can sound happy, sad, or nostalgic. Very singable and memorable.
famousSongs: Let It Be, No Woman No Cry, With or Without You, Someone Like You
commonProgressions.tips: Extremely versatile. Works for entire songs. Can be played in any order. vi-IV-I-V also very common. The ultimate pop progression.

commonProgressions.name: I-vi-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: Classic '50s progression - the doo-wop changes. Foundation of early rock and roll.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Nostalgic, romantic, optimistic. The vi minor creates brief melancholy before resolving.
famousSongs: Stand By Me, Every Breath You Take, Blue Moon, Heart and Soul
commonProgressions.tips: Perfect for retro vibes. vi chord adds emotional color. Can enhance with 7ths (Cmaj7-Am7-Fmaj7-G7) for jazz flavor.

commonProgressions.name: I-vi-ii-V
commonProgressions.description: Jazz standard progression - the rhythm changes. Essential in jazz repertoire.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Sophisticated and smooth. Strong harmonic motion through circle-of-fifths (vi-ii-V-I).
famousSongs: I Got Rhythm, Oleo, Anthropology, Rhythm-a-ning
commonProgressions.tips: Essential for jazz. The ii-V-I creates strong resolution. Practice with 7th chords. Add tensions (9ths, 11ths, 13ths) for sophistication.

commonProgressions.name: ii-V-I
commonProgressions.description: The fundamental jazz progression. The building block of jazz harmony - strongest resolution in tonal music.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Creates powerful resolution. Smooth voice leading. The gold standard for functional harmony.
famousSongs: Autumn Leaves, Satin Doll, Fly Me to the Moon, All the Things You Are
commonProgressions.tips: Master this to unlock jazz. Practice with extensions: Dm9-G13-Cmaj9. V7 can be altered (G7alt). Essential for jazz improvisation.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-vi-V
commonProgressions.description: Optimistic pop/folk progression with smooth voice leading. Very common in singer-songwriter music.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Warm, hopeful, melodically smooth. Gentle emotional arc from bright to contemplative and back.
famousSongs: Hey Soul Sister, Ho Hey, Home, Count on Me
commonProgressions.tips: Perfect for acoustic guitar. Can add 7ths (Cmaj7-Fmaj7-Am7-G7). vi minor adds depth without sadness.

commonProgressions.name: vi-IV-I-V
commonProgressions.description: Modern pop/rock starting on vi minor. Creates immediate emotional depth.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Begins melancholic, builds to hopeful resolution. Perfect for verses that grow emotionally.
commonProgressions.tips: Starting on vi creates immediate emotional impact. Great for verses to brighter choruses. Can loop indefinitely.

commonProgressions.name: I-iii-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: Ascending progression through scale degrees. Less common but emotionally effective.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Builds tension through ascending harmony. iii minor adds sophistication. Creates climbing sensation.
famousSongs: Kiss From a Rose sections, Wouldn't It Be Nice sections
commonProgressions.tips: The iii chord is underused but effective. Smooth voice leading. Great for building sections or bridges.

commonProgressions.name: I-bVII-IV-I
commonProgressions.description: Modal rock progression borrowing bVII from Mixolydian. Creates strong anthemic sound.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Bold, anthemic, powerful. The Bb creates modal flavor. Very popular in 70s-90s rock.
famousSongs: Sweet Child O' Mine sections, Summer of '69 sections
commonProgressions.tips: The Bb is borrowed from C Mixolydian. Creates powerful rock sound. Perfect for arena rock anthems.

commonProgressions.name: vi-ii-V-I
commonProgressions.description: Extended jazz progression combining relative minor with ii-V-I. Smooth descending motion.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Sophisticated and smooth. Longer journey to resolution. Perfect for jazz ballads.
famousSongs: Common in jazz standards and ballads
commonProgressions.tips: Extends ii-V-I with vi start. Practice jazz voicings. Can substitute chords (Am7b5, G7alt). Great for learning harmony.

commonProgressions.name: I-V-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: High-energy rock progression with emphasis on dominant. Creates driving momentum.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Energetic and driving. Creates forward momentum. Double V emphasizes tension and release.
famousSongs: Common in uptempo rock and power pop
commonProgressions.tips: Great for high-energy sections. Repeated V creates urgency. Perfect for choruses and climactic moments.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-I-V
commonProgressions.description: Classic folk and country progression. Simple and timeless.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Stable, traditional, and comforting. Creates cyclical feeling. Perfect for storytelling.
famousSongs: Amazing Grace, This Land Is Your Land, traditional folk songs
commonProgressions.tips: Very stable - keeps returning to I. Perfect for verses in folk and country. Great for traditional and acoustic styles.
